_metadata.description: PharmaGuide public six-pillar Quality Score (decision score) in the 20/20/20/15/15/10 frame. Pillars are category-aware purpose-fit adapters, NOT raw linear remaps: formulation/dose/evidence normalize each archetype to its ACHIEVABLE ceiling (PR4/PR5/PR6); verification saturates hard third-party signals with a fail-open neutral baseline (PR2) and recognizes verified brand/facility-scoped certs as a small non-stacking signal (PR2.1); safety_hygiene is a product-level pillar that routes a maker's Class I recall here, quality-system violations to verification (PR3), and applies a small capped truthfulness deduction for additive/sweetener concerns so flagged products do not show 10/10 safety hygiene. Generic single-bioactive caps prevent clean narrow products from implying elite clinical-formula evidence. Only transparency remains a faithful linear source_dim map (no structural cap to correct). raw_score_v4_100 is audit/math only and is NEVER shown as the shipped score when quality_score_status suppresses display. v4 is the production catalog scoring contract as of export schema 2.0.0.

safety_hygiene_subscale._doc: PR3 + public truthfulness: product-level safety pillar. Clean base (no banned/recalled/watchlist match) → full 10; banned/recalled/watchlist present zeroes the raw safety_hygiene_base → 0. A Class I (critical) manufacturer recall in the last 3y deducts the raw violation magnitude HERE (safety/contamination half of the violation split); non-critical quality-system violations go to the verification pillar instead. B1 additive/sweetener penalties still mainly lower formulation, but they also take a small capped Safety Hygiene deduction so products with additive or glycemic-sweetener concerns do not show 10/10 safety hygiene. B7 overdose remains primarily a Dose-pillar penalty and also gets a small capped Safety Hygiene truthfulness deduction.

clean_label_subscale._doc: STEP 3a: clean-label additive penalty (titanium dioxide / E171 + future EU-banned additives). A flagged additive INFORMS (clean_label_flags_v4) AND takes a SMALL graduated safety_hygiene penalty WITHOUT forcing a CAUTION verdict (CAUTION is the safety gate's lane). penalty = penalty_base (per-additive, from banned_recalled_ingredients.json clean_label.penalty_base) × role_multiplier[role], summed across flagged additives and clamped to max_total_penalty per product. A coating/excipient (role=inactive) is penalized lightly (×0.5); a flagged additive present as an active is penalized harder (×1.5). MAGNITUDES HERE ARE CONSERVATIVE DEFAULTS PENDING USER/ADVISOR SIGN-OFF (spec §7, reports/v4_clean_label_flag_design.md): titanium dioxide as a coating = 2.0 × 0.5 = −1.0 on the /10 pillar. tier_base is a FALLBACK base used only when an entry omits penalty_base.

evidence_subscale._doc: PR6: category-aware evidence fit. The raw evidence dimension's branded-RCT/consensus floor caps a SINGLE ingredient at 18, reserving 19-20 for multi-active breadth. The spec explicitly forbids this ('do not cap single-ingredient evidence just because it is single; a focused product can have better evidence than a blend'). Normalize single-purpose archetypes to an evidence ceiling of 19 so a strong branded/consensus single (KSM-66, creatine, EPA/DHA) earns ~19 by HAVING the evidence — a weak single (low raw evidence) still scores low. 19 not 20 keeps a sliver for the rare perfect-evidence case; multi/breadth archetypes keep 20.

dose_subscale._doc: PR5: category-aware purpose-fit dose. The raw dose dimension caps a single nutrient/botanical in its supplemental/clinical window at ~22 (CAP_SUPPLEMENTAL_WINDOW); the top 3 (→25) is multi-form/completion that single-purpose products can't earn — the same structural pattern as formulation. Normalize each archetype to its achievable APPROPRIATE-dose ceiling so a well-dosed product earns ~19-20. MEGADOSE-SAFE: an overdosed product already gets low raw dose (overdose → half credit) and a sub-clinical one gets proportional-low; normalizing only lifts appropriately-dosed products, never rewards excess.

formulation_subscale._doc: PR4: category-aware purpose-fit formulation. The raw formulation dimension is scored out of a breadth-dependent max (30 for generic/sports) that a SINGLE ingredient structurally cannot reach (A2-A5 = premium-form stacking, synergy, absorption enhancers are multi-ingredient features). Normalize each archetype to its ACHIEVABLE form ceiling so a best-in-class product earns ~19-20 by being the OPTIMAL FORM, not by faking breadth. Computed from the real form-quality components, so a cheap form (magnesium oxide, low A1) still scores low. This is purpose-fit, not premium-form dominance.

formulation_penalties._doc: B1 harmful-additive severity points subtracted from the raw formulation dimension (and re-read, capped at safety_hygiene_subscale.additive_or_sweetener_max_penalty, by the safety_hygiene pillar). Hoisted from generic_formulation.py's hardcoded constants 2026-07-04 so calibration is a config edit, not a code edit. S6 calibration: moderate/high/critical raised to 2/3/4 for real severity separation; LOW held at 0.5 so benign fillers (silicon dioxide, stearates, maltodextrin) stay quiet and clean products don't move. generic_formulation.py reads these at runtime (single source; logic stays in code per the module's §13 lock). test_v4_additive_points_config guards config<->runtime drift. NOTE: the retiring v3 scorer keeps its OWN copy in scoring_config.json (B1_harmful_additives.risk_points, still 3/2/1/0.5) — the two are DIFFERENT models and are not expected to match; do not sync them.

formulation_magnitudes._doc: A1-A6 + B0 formulation-dimension point values, caps, and tier thresholds — hoisted from generic_formulation.py's hardcoded constants 2026-07-04 as a PURE REFACTOR (values unchanged; verified byte-identical score diff on a real-product sample + targeted formulation tests). generic_formulation.py reads these at load time; test_v4_formulation_magnitudes_config pins each to its original value (drift guard). Structural constants (canonical-ID sets, enzyme name list, supp-type enums, the skip-first flag) stay in code — data/logic, not tunable magnitudes. B1 additive points are in the sibling formulation_penalties block.

dose_magnitudes._doc: Dose-dimension caps, windows, thresholds and penalties per category module — hoisted from the score_dose modules 2026-07-04 as a PURE REFACTOR (values unchanged; verified byte-identical score_dose diff on a real-product sample). Each module reads its sub-block via scoring_v4.quality_score_config. Structural constants stay in code. Drift-guarded by test_v4_dose_magnitudes_config.

evidence_magnitudes._doc: Evidence-dimension caps, primary-evidence floors, enrollment/depth bands and effect-direction multipliers per category module — hoisted from the score_evidence modules 2026-07-04 as a PURE REFACTOR (values unchanged; empty score_evidence diff verified, incl. generic's primary-floor path). Structural constants (eligible study-type sets, positioning/relevance sets) stay in code. Drift-guarded by test_v4_evidence_magnitudes_config.

verification_magnitudes._doc: Trust/verification point values, cert-scope points, and manufacturer D1-D5 + violation caps per module — hoisted from the trust/manufacturer/brand-testing/verification-bonus modules 2026-07-04 as a PURE REFACTOR (values unchanged; empty diff verified across score_trust / score_manufacturer_trust / score_manufacturer_violations / score_brand_testing_posture / score_verification_bonus). Structural cert-name sets stay in code; CLASS_I_LOOKBACK_DAYS (3*365) stays in code as a documented window. Drift-guarded by test_v4_verification_magnitudes_config.

transparency_magnitudes._doc: Transparency-dimension disclosure bases, blend (B5) coefficients/multipliers, allergen (B2)/label (B3) points and disease-claim (B6) penalty per module — hoisted from the score_transparency modules 2026-07-04 as a PURE REFACTOR (values unchanged; empty score_transparency diff verified). Regex keyword patterns + the generic-override category set stay in code. Drift-guarded by test_v4_transparency_magnitudes_config.

formulation_variant_magnitudes._doc: Category-specific formulation/profile caps, form-quality points, standardization tiers and botanical dose-band credits — hoisted from the variant score_formulation / botanical / collagen modules 2026-07-04 as a PURE REFACTOR (values unchanged; empty diff verified across all variant + profile entrypoints). Canonical-ID sets stay in code. Drift-guarded by test_v4_formulation_variant_magnitudes_config. (The generic formulation module's magnitudes live in the flat formulation_magnitudes block.)

verification_subscale._doc: PR2/PR2.1: hard third-party signals saturate the cap (over-provisioned, internal sum clamps at 15) so a SUBSET (e.g. one gold cert) maxes it. Self-asserted cGMP is table stakes (near-zero). FAIL OPEN: a product with no third-party cert AND no COA is data-UNKNOWN, not uncertified — it floors at a neutral baseline so the catalog we lack cert data on is not cratered (confidence carries the gap). PR2.1 recognizes verified brand/facility-scoped third-party certs as a small hard signal, weaker than sku/product_line and non-stacking with product-level cert/COA. Product-label/rules-db quality-cert claims get only provisional label_asserted_cert_points unless registry-backed; needs_review stays zero. Soft reputation/region capped at 3; physician/sustainability dropped. The wall truly lifts only when real cert data is sourced (cert_registry task).
